Title: The control of red colour by a family of MYB transcription factors in octoploid strawberry fruits

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Anthocyanins, mainly pelargonidin-3-glucoside in strawberry fruit
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis; possibly early flavonoid/proanthocyanin-related flux at early stages
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FaPAL; FaCHS; FaCHI; Fa4CL; FaF3H; FaDFR; FaANS; FaUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of key early and late flavonoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms canonical MBW complex with bHLH and WD40; FaMYB10-1 interacts with FaWD40 and PavbHLH, whereas FaMYB10-2 cannot
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Endogenous ABA increase during late fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator controlling red fruit colour; decisive functional allele determines anthocyanin accumulation versus colour loss
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low from G1 to W, rises sharply from T to R, maintained high to OR in red strawberry; much lower and not maintained in white strawberry
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression induced strong FaMYB10 transcript accumulation in infiltrated tobacco leaves and white strawberry fruit
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT HyperTrans expression system for transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; cDNA/genomic allele sequencing; yeast two-hybrid; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and white strawberry fruit; anthocyanin metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional FaMYB10-1 promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration; truncated FaMYB10-2 causes loss of anthocyanin accumulation and white fruit phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB10 expression strongly correlates with anthocyanin accumulation during ripening. 2. Red-fruited plants carry FaMYB10-1 encoding a full-length protein, whereas white-fruited plants carry FaMYB10-2 with an 8-bp insertion causing premature termination. 3. FaMYB10-1 forms an MBW complex with FaWD40 and PavbHLH and activates anthocyanin production, while FaMYB10-2 fails to do so. 4. Transient expression of FaMYB10-1 restores/induces anthocyanin accumulation in white strawberry fruit and tobacco leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding red fruit colour and anthocyanin enrichment; allele selection or editing to restore full-length FaMYB10 is promising; avoid truncated FaMYB10-2 allele in breeding for red fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; endogenous ABA status
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaMYB10-2 contains ACTTATAC insertion at position 491, producing a premature stop codon and loss of 54 aa from the C-terminus; FaMYB10-2 genomic sequences lack introns, suggesting origin via reverse transcription/transposition

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flesh/receptacle; possibly seeds per discussion
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FaPAL; FaCHS; FaCHI; Fa4CL; FaDFR; FaANS; FaUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Proposed activation of structural genes in this study
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation, likely as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed putative MBW activation complex with bHLH and WD40
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FabHLH3/FaWD40 (proposed); NA experimentally validated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributory positive regulator associated with anthocyanin index, but less decisive than FaMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreases from G1 to T then rises from T to peak at R/OR; higher in red strawberry at some stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; cDNA/genomic allele sequencing; PCA correlation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration in this study, though prior reports suggested negative correlation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB1 transcript levels are higher in red than white fruit at key late stages. 2. PCA linked FaMYB1 with anthocyanin accumulation and expression of anthocyanin structural genes. 3. FaMYB1 allelic variants were found in both white and red varieties, so FaMYB1 sequence variation does not explain white fruit colour. 4. The study proposes a positive role for FaMYB1 in anthocyanin regulation in octoploid strawberry.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible supportive regulator for anthocyanin enhancement, but functional role is less certain than FaMYB10 and should be deployed cautiously
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Conflicting functional interpretation with previous studies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaMYB1 role is ambiguous because prior literature suggested negative correlation with anthocyanins, whereas this paper infers a positive role

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to flavonoid composition of fruit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with activation of proanthocyanidin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, proposed via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed MBW complex with bHLH and WD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator of proanthocyanidin accumulation, especially early fruit stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest at G1 and decreases rapidly during development; relatively higher in white fruit at T and R than red fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; PCA correlation analysis; phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with proanthocyanidin accumulation rather than red anthocyanin coloration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB9 expression peaks early in fruit development and declines thereafter. 2. PCA links FaMYB9 with proanthocyanidin/proanthocyanidin precursor accumulation and with FaLAR/FaFLS expression. 3. The gene is proposed to regulate proanthocyanidin biosynthesis rather than ripe fruit anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate proanthocyanidin composition in early fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited relevance to ripe red colour; likely suppressed at later stages
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference based largely on expression/PCA/phylogeny rather than direct transactivation assays in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to flavonoid composition of fruit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with activation of proanthocyanidin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, proposed via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed MBW complex with bHLH and WD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator of proanthocyanidin accumulation, especially early fruit stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest at G1 and decreases rapidly during development; relatively higher in white fruit at T and R than red fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; PCA correlation analysis; phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with proanthocyanidin accumulation rather than red anthocyanin coloration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB11 expression is strongest early in fruit development and declines later. 2. PCA and phylogenetic analyses support a role in proanthocyanidin biosynthesis. 3. The gene is not a principal determinant of ripe red colour but contributes to the proanthocyanidin branch.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for adjusting proanthocyanidin content/composition in developing fruit
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited direct contribution to ripe anthocyanin-rich red colour
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference based largely on expression/PCA/phylogeny rather than direct functional assays in this paper
